name: moltis
description: A Rust-native claw you can trust. One binary — sandboxed, secure, auditable. Voice, memory, MCP tools, and multi-channel access built-in.

commit.message:
feat(release): migrate to date-based versioning (YYYYMMDD.NN) (#394)

* feat(release): migrate to date-based versioning (YYYYMMDD.NN)

Replace semver with date-based version format. Cargo.toml stays at
static 0.1.0; the real version is injected at build time via the
MOLTIS_VERSION env var and exposed through moltis_config::VERSION.

- Add central VERSION constant in crates/config/src/version.rs
- Replace all env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION") with moltis_config::VERSION
- Rewrite update_check.rs to handle both date and semver formats,
  treating any date version as newer than any semver version so
  existing users see the upgrade
- Update prepare-release.sh to accept YYYYMMDD.NN and auto-compute
  next sequence number from git tags
- Update release.yml: new tag pattern, validate-tag job, inject
  MOLTIS_VERSION into all build steps, remove Cargo version checks
- Update homebrew.yml, cliff.toml, Dockerfile, justfile, CLAUDE.md

Entire-Checkpoint: 569a35f15918

* fix(release): broader sed pattern for deploy templates, fix octal bug

- Use `[0-9][0-9.]*` in deploy template sed patterns so they match
  both existing semver (0.10.18) and new date-based (20260311.01)
  versions during migration
- Force base-10 comparison (10#$seq) in prepare-release.sh to avoid
  bash octal errors with sequence numbers 08/09

Entire-Checkpoint: b37b276b099c

* fix(ci): revert CHANGELOG.md edit to pass changelog guard

The header update will be applied automatically by git-cliff on the
next release via the updated cliff.toml template.

Entire-Checkpoint: 2e1f7efea614

commit.message:
feat(providers): add Responses API support to GitHub Copilot provider (#393)

* feat(providers): add Responses API support to GitHub Copilot provider

Newer OpenAI models (gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-pro, gpt-5.2-pro) only support
the Responses API (/responses), returning HTTP 400 with
unsupported_api_for_model when called via /chat/completions.

Add model-based routing with error-based fallback:
- Known models (gpt-5.4*, gpt-5.2-pro, codex-*) dispatch directly to
  /responses for both streaming (SSE) and non-streaming requests
- Unknown models that return unsupported_api_for_model errors
  automatically fall back from /chat/completions to /responses

Extract shared Responses API utilities to openai_compat.rs so both
OpenAI and GitHub Copilot providers can reuse them:
- split_responses_instructions_and_input (moved from OpenAiProvider)
- responses_output_index (extracted from ws_output_index)
- ResponsesStreamState, process_responses_sse_line,
  finalize_responses_stream, parse_responses_completion (new)

Closes #392

Entire-Checkpoint: f20b03984347

* fix(providers): use case-insensitive matching in Responses API error detection

Address review feedback: is_responses_api_required_error now normalizes
to lowercase before checking substrings, consistent with the analogous
is_chat_endpoint_unsupported_model_error in openai.rs.

Entire-Checkpoint: ad1ce8e02970

commit.message:
fix(agents): include tool_result messages in LLM conversation history (#389)

* fix(agents): include tool_result messages in LLM conversation history

Persisted tool_result entries were skipped when rebuilding chat history
for the LLM, so on subsequent turns the model could not see its own
tool outputs — leading to hallucinations, repeated tool calls, and
broken prefix caching.

Convert tool_result → tool in values_to_chat_messages() and remove the
now-redundant conversion in full_context().

Closes #319

Entire-Checkpoint: 92101f312ace

* fix(agents): update existing test to expect tool_result inclusion

The convert_skips_tool_result_entries test now expects 3 messages
(including the converted tool message) instead of 2.

Entire-Checkpoint: ad8f04c3aebd

* fix(agents): skip orphan tool results and handle string values

- Track tool_call IDs from assistant messages; only emit tool/tool_result
  messages that have a matching assistant tool_call, preventing provider
  API errors from orphan tool results (e.g. from explicit /sh commands).
- Use as_str() before to_string() on tool_result values to avoid
  wrapping plain strings in JSON quotes.
- Update existing tests to include matching assistant tool_calls.

Entire-Checkpoint: 12f04195cd57

commit.message:
fix(gateway): retain proxy shutdown sender to prevent immediate proxy exit (#368)

The `watch::Sender` for the trusted-network proxy was declared inside the
`#[cfg(feature = "trusted-network")]` block and dropped at block exit,
causing the proxy task to receive an immediate shutdown signal. Move the
sender into `PreparedGateway` so it lives as long as the server.

Fixes #367

commit.message:
fix(cron): add delay_ms to avoid LLM computing absolute timestamps (#377)

The 'at' schedule kind previously required the LLM to supply at_ms as
an absolute epoch millisecond value. LLMs reliably miscalculate this
(training-data clock skew means 'now' is off by months or years),
causing one-shot jobs to be scheduled in the past and never fire.

Add delay_ms (aliases: delayMs, delay, in, in_ms, offset_ms) as a
server-resolved relative offset: the tool computes now_ms + delay_ms
at call time, so the LLM only needs to express durations (e.g. 600000
for 10 minutes) rather than absolute timestamps.

Existing at_ms is preserved for callers that have a real absolute time.

commit.message:
fix(whatsapp): sled persistence, graceful shutdown, and review fixes (#387)

* fix(whatsapp): sled persistence + graceful shutdown + account state lifecycle

* fix: address PR #387 review feedback

- Revert service file generators to only pass `--timeout` flag;
  `node run` reads connection params from node.json (Codex review)
- Add xml_escape for plist values to prevent malformed XML (Greptile)
- Reject invalid WhatsApp configs instead of persisting them; only
  suppress UnknownAccount errors as warnings (Codex review)
- Use read lock instead of write lock in mirror_connected since
  AtomicBool::store only needs &self (Greptile review)

Entire-Checkpoint: c298ddf385d9

* fix(whatsapp): abort bot task on cancel and update startup-bound config

- Abort the JoinHandle on cancellation instead of dropping it, then
  await its completion so the sled store is fully released before
  signalling shutdown done (fixes sled lock race on stop/start)
- Update OtpState cooldown in update_account_config so startup-bound
  fields take effect without a full restart
- Add OtpState::set_cooldown() to channels crate

Entire-Checkpoint: 6365b959e66a

---------

Co-authored-by: JulioSM98 <juliosejas98@gmail.com>

commit.message:
fix(auth): bypass auth for local API requests during onboarding (#386)

* fix(auth): bypass auth for local API requests during onboarding

During first-time onboarding, the STT "Test" button fails with 401
because transcribeAudio() uses HTTP fetch (POST /api/sessions/{key}/upload)
which goes through auth_gate, unlike the WS-based voice config RPCs.

Add a narrow bypass in auth_gate's Unauthorized branch: when the request
is local, targets /api/* or /ws/*, and onboarding hasn't completed yet
(wizard_status reports onboarded=false), allow the request through with
Loopback identity.

Also fix a pre-existing missing .send().await.unwrap() in the /ws
public route test and two nightly rustfmt formatting issues.

Closes #378

Entire-Checkpoint: 333257de8c57

* fix: resolve pre-existing clippy warnings in node-host service

Prefix unused `config` params with underscore and replace `vec!` with
array literal in `generate_launchd_plist` / `generate_systemd_unit`.

Entire-Checkpoint: 6b182a1aca6f

* style: fix nightly rustfmt formatting in node-host service

Entire-Checkpoint: cc7596feb984

* fix: revert NoopOnboardingService and node-host changes

Revert NoopOnboardingService to not include "onboarded" field — the
chat_ui test depends on noop returning a not-onboarded state.

Instead, change the auth bypass default from unwrap_or(false) to
unwrap_or(true): when the onboarded field is absent (noop service)
or wizard_status() fails, assume onboarded (safe default = require
auth). The bypass only activates when onboarded is explicitly false.

Revert node-host service.rs to main — the clippy warnings and test
failure are pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

Entire-Checkpoint: a6217c77e389

* fix(node-host): wire ServiceConfig fields into launchd/systemd templates

The generate_launchd_plist() and generate_systemd_unit() functions
accepted a ServiceConfig parameter but never used it, causing clippy
unused-variable warnings. Wire the config fields (gateway-url,
device-token, node-id, name, working-dir, timeout) into the generated
service templates as CLI arguments.

Entire-Checkpoint: dc2070e5181f

* fix: address PR review comments

- Restrict onboarding auth bypass to narrow path set (sessions, bootstrap,
  gon, tailscale, ws) instead of all /api/* and /ws/* paths
- Add test verifying privileged endpoints remain protected during onboarding
- Consolidate duplicate test helpers into single start_server_with_onboarding()
- Fix node-host: only pass --timeout to service templates (the only flag
  `moltis node run` accepts); fix pre-existing broken tests that asserted
  config fields in template output

Entire-Checkpoint: 38493b795e8f

commit.message:
fix(config): write IDENTITY.md and SOUL.md to agents/main/ instead of root (#384)

* fix(config): write IDENTITY.md and SOUL.md to agents/main/ instead of root

Settings UI and onboarding wrote these files to `data_dir/` root, but the
loaders check `data_dir/agents/main/` first.  When agent-level files
already existed, updates via the UI were silently ignored at runtime.

- Add `save_soul_for_agent()` mirroring existing `save_identity_for_agent()`
- Switch onboarding wizard and settings handlers to save to `agents/main/`
- Fix `identity_update` response to read soul from `load_soul_for_agent`
- Add tests for the new `save_soul_for_agent` function

Closes #376

Entire-Checkpoint: 5e2d7e09626f

* fix: rustfmt and missing .send().await in auth_middleware test

Pre-existing issues on main that blocked local-validate:
- Format node_commands.rs and auth_middleware.rs (nightly rustfmt)
- Add missing .send().await.unwrap() in ws test assertion

Entire-Checkpoint: f618feb9967b

* fix: unused variables and useless vec in node-host service

Pre-existing clippy warnings on main:
- Prefix unused `config` params with underscore
- Replace `vec!` with array literal where Vec is unnecessary

Entire-Checkpoint: ea8f8bbc14af

* fix(node-host): pass ServiceConfig fields as launchd plist arguments

generate_launchd_plist was ignoring the config parameter, so gateway_url,
device_token, timeout, and optional fields (node_id, display_name,
working_dir) were never included in the plist ProgramArguments.

Entire-Checkpoint: 5333ef27d367

* fix(projects): disable GPG signing in worktree test repos

init_test_repo creates a temp git repo and commits, but global
commit.gpgsign=true causes the commit to fail when pinentry cannot
prompt (CI, non-interactive terminals). Set commit.gpgsign=false
in the test repo config.

Entire-Checkpoint: 56f6e8c3d0b9

* fix: address PR review feedback for consistent read/write paths

- Fix inline save_soul() in identity_update to use save_soul_for_agent
- Fix load_soul_for_agent to respect empty file as explicit clear
  (don't fall back to root when agents/main/SOUL.md exists but is empty)
- Revert launchd plist to only pass --timeout (the only valid CLI flag);
  node run reads other config from node.json at runtime
- Fix Swift bridge to use load/save_soul_for_agent("main")
- Fix instance_slug to use load_identity_for_agent("main")

Entire-Checkpoint: 54c271bc6827

* fix: replace all remaining root-level identity/soul read/write calls

Migrate wizard.rs, sandbox_commands.rs, swift-bridge, agent_persona.rs,
and services.rs write_soul_for_agent to use agent-scoped variants.
Simplify write_soul_for_agent by delegating to save_soul_for_agent.

Entire-Checkpoint: 4e17187c786b

* fix: update identity_update_partial test soul path to agents/main/

Entire-Checkpoint: fc8f55c003d0

* fix: preserve explicit identity clears with empty file sentinel

When all identity fields are cleared, write an empty IDENTITY.md
instead of deleting it. load_identity_for_agent("main") now checks
file existence before falling back to root, preventing stale root
data from resurfacing after a clear.

Entire-Checkpoint: 200c39a600e4

commit.message:
docs: clarify Docker socket fallback behavior (#388)

The Docker socket section contained contradictory statements: one said
shell commands would "fail" without the socket, another said Moltis
would run in "no sandbox" mode. Neither was accurate — Moltis actually
falls back to the restricted-host sandbox automatically.

Replace both with a clear description of the actual behavior and link
to the existing sandbox docs for details.

Closes #351

* fix(gateway): add /ws route and bypass auth middleware for node connections (#381)

Add /ws as a public WebSocket endpoint so remote nodes can connect
using device-token authentication at the WebSocket protocol layer.

- Register /ws route in both build_gateway_base variants
- Add /ws to is_public_path() in auth_middleware
- Add test verifying /ws bypasses auth middleware

* fix(web): correct node connection string and command in nodes page (#380)

- Use location.host instead of gon.get('port') + location.hostname for
  the WebSocket URL, matching the pattern used by all other pages
  (ws-connect.js, page-settings.js, page-terminal.js)
- Change generated command from 'moltis node run' to 'moltis node add'
  to match the actual CLI subcommand

endpoint as a public path (device token auth at WebSocket protocol

node.json.

commit.message:
fix(web): break redirect loop when accessing via Tailscale Serve (#356)

* fix(web): break redirect loop when accessing via Tailscale Serve (#350)

When accessing remotely via Tailscale Serve (HTTPS→HTTP proxy), the
connection is non-local so auth returns SetupRequired. The JS redirected
to /onboarding, but the server saw onboarding was complete and redirected
back to / — infinite loop.

Fix: don't redirect away from /onboarding when auth setup is pending,
and show only the auth step (not the full wizard) in that scenario.

* chore: add entire settings with auto-summarize enabled

Entire-Checkpoint: 1644caeed36c

* chore: upgrade biome config to 2.4.6

Migrate schema version, move noUnusedExpressions from nursery to
suspicious (promoted in 2.4.x), remove stale suppression comment,
and drop unused watchPageErrors import.

Entire-Checkpoint: 45103f6f2eb5

* fix(web): show setup-required page for remote access without auth (#350)

Replace the previous approach (auth-only onboarding step) with a safer
design: when auth is not configured and a remote client connects, the
auth middleware redirects to /setup-required — a static page telling
the user to configure auth locally or via CLI.

This prevents both the infinite redirect loop (the original bug) and
the security issue where a remote user could claim the instance by
setting up auth before the owner.

Entire-Checkpoint: 7b0b05ac2439

* style: apply rustfmt to auth_middleware

Entire-Checkpoint: 201f256d35f6

* test(gateway): update onboarding auth test for setup-required redirect

The test previously expected /onboarding to serve the page for remote
connections during setup. Now it correctly expects a redirect to
/setup-required.

Entire-Checkpoint: 7a2feec44213

* test(web): improve setup-required coverage and redirect stale bookmarks

Add guard to /setup-required handler that redirects to /login when auth
is already configured, preventing stale bookmarks from showing a
misleading page. Add integration tests covering local pass-through,
remote redirect for root path, public-path accessibility, post-setup
redirect, and a unit test for the SetupRequired template rendering.

Entire-Checkpoint: 854bee72da09

* fix(gateway): allow /onboarding through for remote during setup

Remote /onboarding must pass through the auth gate during setup so
the onboarding page can render its own setup-code auth flow (step 0).
Only non-onboarding page paths redirect to /setup-required for remote
connections without auth configured (#350).

Update E2E onboarding-auth test to navigate directly to /onboarding
since / now redirects to /setup-required for remote during setup.

Entire-Checkpoint: 0116f75fbec8

commit.message:
fix(chat): skip duplicate text fallback when TTS disabled and voice streamed (#373)

* fix(chat): skip duplicate text fallback when TTS disabled and voice streamed (#371)

When a voice message is received with TTS disabled, the text was already
delivered via edit-in-place streaming but the voice reply path fell
through to send_text() after build_tts_payload returned None. Add a
text_already_streamed guard to both the Telegram and generic None
branches in deliver_channel_replies_to_targets.

* fix(chat): send logbook follow-up for non-Telegram channels in voice+no-TTS path

Address Greptile review: the generic channel branch was silently dropping
the logbook when text_already_streamed was true. Mirror the Telegram
branch by sending send_html logbook follow-up. Add two tests covering
logbook delivery for both Telegram and non-Telegram voice+no-TTS+streamed
scenarios.

fix(web): allow deleting cron sessions from chat sidebar (#357)

* fix(web): allow deleting cron sessions from chat sidebar

Remove the isCron guard from the delete button in session-header so
cron sessions can be deleted like regular sessions. The backend already
supports this — only the UI was blocking it.

Closes #342

* chore: add .entire directory gitignore

* chore: migrate biome config to 2.4.6

Update schema version and move noUnusedExpressions from nursery to
suspicious (promoted in biome 2.4). Apply auto-format fixes.

* fix(web): use correct selector for cron tab in E2E test

The tab uses data-tab="cron" not data-session-tab="cron".

* fix(web): address review feedback on cron delete E2E test

Remove redundant watchPageErrors call (use navigateAndWait return value)
and add message-loaded assertion before clicking delete to prevent flaky
race condition.

* fix(web): create cron session in DB before testing delete flow

The system-event RPC only broadcasts a WebSocket event without
persisting a session to the database. Add a sessions.switch RPC call
first to create the cron session in SQLite so fetchSessions() can
find it and render it in the session list.

commit.message:
feat(providers): add reasoning effort support for models with extended thinking (#363)

* feat(providers): add reasoning effort support for models with extended thinking (#347)

Models that support reasoning (Claude 3.7+/4.5+, OpenAI o-series, Gemini 2.5,
DeepSeek R1) now appear with reasoning variants in all model selectors:
"Claude Opus 4.5 (low reasoning)", "(medium reasoning)", "(high reasoning)".

Model IDs use @-suffix convention (e.g. claude-opus-4-5@reasoning-high).
ProviderRegistry::get() transparently resolves the suffix and applies the
matching ReasoningEffort to the provider via with_reasoning_effort().

Provider-specific request injection:
- Anthropic: thinking.budget_tokens (4096/10240/32768 by effort level)
- OpenAI: reasoning.effort field for o-series models

Also configurable per agent preset via reasoning_effort in moltis.toml.

* style(web): apply biome 2.4.6 auto-fixes

Entire-Checkpoint: efe666481f5a

* chore: update biome schema to 2.4.6 and fix nursery rules

noUnusedExpressions graduated from nursery to suspicious in biome 2.4.x.

* fix: collapse nested if in reasoning_effort validation (clippy)

* fix: collapse nested if in apply_thinking (clippy)

* fix(providers): address Greptile review issues for reasoning effort

- Split OpenAI apply_reasoning_effort into two methods: apply_reasoning_effort_chat
  (top-level "reasoning_effort" field for Chat Completions API) and
  apply_reasoning_effort_responses (nested "reasoning.effort" for Responses API)
- Move ReasoningEffort enum from moltis-agents to moltis-config for type safety;
  AgentPreset.reasoning_effort is now Option<ReasoningEffort> instead of Option<String>
- Add early-return optimization in raw_model_id when no '@' separator present
- Warn when preset reasoning_effort overrides model-ID @reasoning-* suffix
- Remove redundant string-based semantic validation (now handled by serde)

* fix(providers): group reasoning variants adjacent to their base model

Instead of appending all reasoning variants at the end of the model
list, interleave each model's variants immediately after its base
entry so related options stay together in the selector.

commit.message:
fix(prompt): omit sandbox/node info from runtime prompt when disabled (#362)

* fix(prompt): omit sandbox and node info from runtime prompt when disabled (#360)

When sandbox is disabled or unavailable, remove all sandbox-related
context from the runtime prompt to prevent the LLM from hallucinating
sandbox usage. Use simplified host-only exec routing guidance when
sandbox is not active.

Closes #360

* chore: add .entire directory

* chore: trigger PR sync

* fix(scripts): auto-build CSS when style.css is missing in local-validate

Worktrees and fresh clones don't have the generated Tailwind output,
causing clippy/build to fail on include_str!("assets/style.css").

* fix(prompt): make sudo hint conditional and document session_override discard

- Only include the `sudo_non_interactive` hint in exec routing guidance
  when sudo is actually available on the host, avoiding dead text that
  could confuse the LLM.
- Clarify in a comment that discarding `session_override` when sandbox
  is disabled is intentional (its only consumer is the omitted prompt
  line).

* fix(prompt): add approval hint to host-only guidance and test sudo path

- Add "may require approval" to EXEC_ROUTING_GUIDANCE_HOST_ONLY, matching
  the sandbox-aware guidance so the LLM expects approval dialogs.
- Add test for host-only + sudo_non_interactive=true combination.
- Wrap `just build-css` in run_check for consistent error reporting.

Addresses greptile review feedback on PR #362.

* fix(prompt): reorder sudo hint before sandbox closing line and add test

- Extract EXEC_ROUTING_SANDBOX_CLOSING from EXEC_ROUTING_GUIDANCE_SANDBOX
  so the sudo hint appears before the "don't be surprised" closing
  directive, preserving logical ordering.
- Add test for sandbox-enabled + no sudo combination to verify the sudo
  hint is correctly absent.

Addresses greptile review feedback on PR #362.

* style(prompt): fix rustfmt nightly formatting for EXEC_ROUTING_SANDBOX_CLOSING

fix: install rustls CryptoProvider before channel startup (#336)

When running with `--no-tls` (e.g. behind a reverse proxy),
`install_default()` was only called inside the TLS cert-loading path
which is skipped entirely. Channel plugins like Slack that make
outbound HTTPS connections via hyper-rustls then panic with:

Move the `install_default()` call to early in `prepare_gateway()` so
it runs unconditionally before any channel account is started.

feat(nodes): add multi-node support with device pairing, remote exec, and UI (#291)

* feat(nodes): add multi-node support with device pairing, remote exec, and UI

Implement the full multi-node architecture allowing headless nodes to
connect to the gateway via WebSocket, authenticate with device tokens,
and execute commands remotely.

Key changes:
- SQLite-backed PairingStore for persistent device pairing with SHA-256
  hashed tokens (mdt_ prefix), replacing in-memory PairingState
- Device token authentication in WS handshake (new auth path)
- NodeExecProvider trait + GatewayNodeExecProvider bridge for routing
  exec commands to remote nodes via node.invoke/system.run
- New moltis-node-host crate: WS client that connects as a headless
  node and handles system.run/system.which commands
- CLI: `moltis node run` and `moltis nodes list` commands
- Web UI: Nodes settings page with connected/paired/pending tabs
- Config: tools.exec.host ("local"/"node") and tools.exec.node fields
- Environment variable security filtering for remote execution

* style: apply rustfmt (pinned nightly) to new multi-node files

* style: apply biome 2.x formatting to JS files

* fix: collapse nested if-let blocks (clippy::collapsible_if)

* test(e2e): add Nodes to expected settings sidebar order

* feat(nodes): add agent node tools, provider discovery, richer telemetry, and chat node selector

- Add NodeInfoProvider trait and three AgentTools (nodes_list, nodes_describe,
  nodes_select) so the LLM can query and target connected devices
- Add system.providers command to node-host for discovering Ollama and
  env-based LLM providers on remote nodes
- Extend telemetry with disk usage, installed runtimes, and provider info
- Add chat toolbar node selector dropdown (mirrors model selector pattern)
- Add node-store Preact signal store and nodes-selector component
- Wire nodes.set_session RPC to persist per-session node binding
- Add session_node_id migration for sessions table
- Add deferred provider registry startup (skip network, backfill later)
- Add pairing token rotation and revocation RPCs
- Add E2E tests for node selector component

* feat(nodes): add node service install/uninstall/status/stop/restart commands

Add OS service management so the node-host survives reboots:
- macOS: launchd user agent (~/Library/LaunchAgents/org.moltis.node.plist)
- Linux: systemd user unit (~/.config/systemd/user/moltis-node.service)
- Persists connection params to ~/.moltis/node.json
- KeepAlive/Restart on failure with throttle for both platforms
- `moltis node logs` prints the log file path

* feat(service): add moltis service install/uninstall/status/stop/restart

Install the moltis gateway as an OS service that survives reboots:
- macOS: launchd user agent (~/Library/LaunchAgents/org.moltis.gateway.plist)
- Linux: systemd user unit (~/.config/systemd/user/moltis.service)
- Optional --bind, --port, --log-level flags baked into the service
- KeepAlive/Restart on failure with 10s throttle
- `moltis service logs` prints the log file path

* docs: add Multi-Node and Service Management documentation

- Add docs/src/nodes.md covering pairing, running nodes, node selector,
  telemetry, agent tools, security, and CLI reference
- Add docs/src/service.md covering gateway service install/manage/uninstall
  with comparison table between `moltis service` and `moltis node`
- Remove misleading stop/restart/status from `moltis node` — nodes are
  stateless from the gateway's perspective and don't have a start/stop
  lifecycle; the service daemon is managed via launchctl/systemctl directly
- Update SUMMARY.md with new pages under Features

* refactor(node): rename node commands to add/remove/status

Replace implementation-leaking `run`/`install`/`uninstall` with user-facing
`add`/`remove`/`status`:
- `moltis node add --host <url> --token <tok>` installs as a service by
  default, pass --foreground for interactive mode
- `moltis node remove` disconnects and removes the service
- `moltis node status` shows gateway URL, name, and service state
- Update Multi-Node and Service Management docs to match

* refactor(node): restructure CLI to prepare/add/list/remove/status/logs

Rename subcommands to match actual user intent:
- `prepare` generates a device token (CLI equiv of web UI "Generate Token")
- `add --host --token` joins this machine to a gateway as a node
- `list` queries gateway for connected nodes
- `remove` disconnects and removes the node service
- `status` / `logs` for local node diagnostics

Remove `nodes_commands.rs` (absorbed into `node list`). Add
`gateway_rpc()` helper for one-shot CLI-to-gateway HTTP calls.

* refactor(node): rename prepare to generate-token for clarity

* fix(web): add spacing under Nodes page heading to match other settings pages

* perf(sessions): skip re-sending history when client cache is current

When switching sessions the server previously always sent the full chat
history, even when the client already had it cached. This could transfer
13+ MB on every tab switch for large sessions.

The client now sends cached_message_count with the switch request. If it
matches the server's messageCount the response contains an empty history
array and historyCacheHit: true, letting the client keep its local cache.

* fix(auth): detect WebSocket 401 and redirect to login

When the WebSocket upgrade is rejected (e.g. 401 from a non-local host),
the client previously retried indefinitely with no user feedback.

Now, if onclose fires without onopen ever succeeding, the client checks
/api/auth/status. If unauthenticated it redirects to /login; if setup is
required it redirects to /onboarding. Transient failures still reconnect
normally.

docs: explain why Anthropic OAuth is not supported (#312)

Anthropic does not offer an OAuth program for third-party tools. Their
OAuth flow and create_api_key endpoint are restricted to Claude Code and
Claude.ai only, enforced server-side since January 2026. Using their
client_id from a third-party application violates the Consumer ToS.

Add a dedicated FAQ page under LLM Providers documenting the policy,
the technical reasons, and the recommended API key setup so we can
link to it from Discord when users ask.

commit.message:
fix(gateway): reduce idle CPU from metrics loop and log broadcast feedback

- Change metrics collection interval from 10s to 30s (standard Prometheus
  scrape interval), cutting background work (render + parse + SQLite write
  + JSON serialize + WS broadcast) by 3x.
- Fix log broadcast feedback loop: when RUST_LOG=debug, broadcasting a
  log entry emits a debug log which gets re-captured and re-broadcast
  infinitely, pegging the CPU. Skip entries from moltis_gateway::broadcast.
- Update cleanup counter threshold (360→120) to maintain hourly cadence.
- Update all comments and docs referencing the old 10-second interval.

for self-hosted or alternate CDN overrides. Update CSP to allow the

commit.message:
feat(local-llm): allow arbitrary HuggingFace model IDs for MLX models

Accept any HuggingFace repo ID (e.g. mlx-community/Qwen3.5-4B-MLX-4bit)
as a model ID for the local-llm provider, instead of requiring models to
be in the built-in catalog. The provider detects org/repo format IDs and
downloads them directly from HuggingFace.

Also fixes the Settings web UI flow: configure_custom now uses the HF
repo ID directly as the model_id for MLX models so the provider can
resolve and download them automatically.

Closes #308

Allow all page paths through in SetupRequired state — the SPA handles

fix(web): skip npm install when TAILWINDCSS binary is provided

The build script unconditionally ran npm install before checking the
TAILWINDCSS env var, causing CI to fail with exit code 127 on the CUDA
container which has no npm. Move binary resolution first so standalone
binaries (set via TAILWINDCSS) bypass npm entirely.

commit.message:
feat(browser): add container_host for Docker-in-Docker connectivity (#300)

* feat(browser): add container_host config for Docker-in-Docker browser connectivity

When Moltis runs inside a Docker container and spawns a sibling browser
container, 127.0.0.1 only reaches the Moltis container's loopback — not
the host where the browser port is mapped. Add a configurable
container_host field (default "127.0.0.1") so users can set it to
"host.docker.internal" or the Docker bridge IP to reach the browser.

* style(browser): fix rustfmt formatting

commit.message:
fix(providers): use Ollama capabilities for tool support detection (#301)

* fix(providers): use Ollama capabilities field for tool support detection

The hardcoded OLLAMA_NATIVE_TOOL_FAMILIES whitelist caused ~50% tool
call failures for models not in the list (e.g. MiniMax M2.5) by forcing
them into text mode, even though Ollama's /api/show reports native tool
support via the capabilities field.

- Read capabilities from OllamaShowResponse, fall back to family
  whitelist only for pre-0.5.x Ollama without capabilities
- Sanitize tool names (trim whitespace, strip quotes) before registry
  lookup to handle models that wrap names in quotes
- Add <invoke> XML format parser for models that emit tool calls as
  <invoke name="tool"><arg name="key">value</arg></invoke>
- Add "invoke" and "tool_calls" to response sanitizer INTERNAL_TAGS

Closes #281

* fix(providers): collapse nested if per clippy collapsible_if lint

* test(agents,providers): add regression and edge-case tests for tool calling

Backward compatibility:
- Verify fenced, XML function, and bare JSON formats still parse
  identically after adding the invoke parser
- Verify sanitize_tool_name is a no-op on all real production tool names
- Verify OllamaShowResponse deserializes from both old (no capabilities)
  and new (with capabilities) Ollama JSON

Edge cases:
- invoke: no args, unclosed tag, missing name, empty name, JSON arg
  values, multiline values, mixed with fenced blocks, multiple blocks
- sanitize_tool_name: empty, only-quotes, internal quotes, single quotes
- response sanitizer: invoke/tool_calls tags stripped, prose "invoke"
  preserved, tool_call recovery unaffected by new INTERNAL_TAGS
- resolve_ollama_tool_mode: no probe result, explicit overrides
  capabilities, single "tools" capability, deserialization roundtrip

* fix(web): install node_modules before resolving tailwindcss binary

The npm install was only running in the fallback npx branch, so when a
global tailwindcss CLI was found the local tailwindcss package was never
installed. The CLI needs the local package to resolve CSS imports like
`@import "tailwindcss"`, causing failures in fresh worktrees/clones.

Move the node_modules check before binary resolution so it runs
unconditionally.

commit.message:
refactor(gateway): fetch updates from releases manifest instead of GitHub API

Replace direct GitHub releases API polling with a website-hosted
releases.json manifest. This decouples update announcements from
GitHub releases, so broken or draft releases are never surfaced.

The manifest supports stable and unstable channels — pre-release
builds check unstable, stable builds check stable. All fetch errors
(404, parse failure, network) are silently ignored.

Config field renamed: server.update_repository_url → server.update_releases_url
Default URL: https://www.moltis.org/releases.json

commit.message:
fix(e2e): wait for session history render before DOM injection in chat-abort

The "aborted broadcast cleans up UI state" test was flaky because it
injected a fake #thinkingIndicator into #messages before the
sessions.switch RPC response arrived. When renderHistory() then
cleared chatMsgBox.textContent, the injected element disappeared.

Wait for state.sessionSwitchInProgress and state.chatBatchLoading
to be false in beforeEach, ensuring history rendering is complete
before any test injects DOM elements.

readme_html:
Moltis — A Rust-native claw you can trust

One binary — sandboxed, secure, yours.

Installation • Comparison • Architecture • Security • Features • How It Works • Contributing

Moltis recently hit the front page of Hacker News. Please open an issue for any friction at all. I'm focused on making Moltis excellent.

Secure by design — Your keys never leave your machine. Every command runs in a sandboxed container, never on your host.

Your hardware — Runs on a Mac Mini, a Raspberry Pi, or any server you own. One Rust binary, no Node.js, no npm, no runtime.

Full-featured — Voice, memory, scheduling, Telegram, Discord, browser automation, MCP servers — all built-in. No plugin marketplace to get supply-chain attacked through.

Auditable — The agent loop + provider model fits in ~5K lines. The core (excluding the optional web UI) is ~196K lines across 46 modular crates you can audit independently, with 3,100+ tests and zero unsafe code*.

Installation

# One-liner install script (macOS / Linux)
curl -fsSL https://www.moltis.org/install.sh | sh

# macOS / Linux via Homebrew
brew install moltis-org/tap/moltis

# Docker (multi-arch: amd64/arm64)
docker pull ghcr.io/moltis-org/moltis:latest

# Or build from source
cargo install moltis --git https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis

Comparison

OpenClaw PicoClaw NanoClaw ZeroClaw Moltis
Language TypeScript Go TypeScript Rust Rust
Agent loop ~430K LoC Small ~500 LoC ~3.4K LoC ~5K LoC (runner.rs + model.rs)
Full codebase — — — 1,000+ tests ~124K LoC (2,300+ tests)
Runtime Node.js + npm Single binary Node.js Single binary (3.4 MB) Single binary (44 MB)
Sandbox App-level — Docker Docker Docker + Apple Container
Memory safety GC GC GC Ownership Ownership, zero unsafe*
Auth Basic API keys None Token + OAuth Password + Passkey + API keys + Vault
Voice I/O Plugin — — — Built-in (15+ providers)
MCP Yes — — — Yes (stdio + HTTP/SSE)
Hooks Yes (limited) — — — 15 event types
Skills Yes (store) Yes Yes Yes Yes (+ OpenClaw Store)
Memory/RAG Plugin — Per-group SQLite + FTS SQLite + FTS + vector

* unsafe is denied workspace-wide. The only exceptions are opt-in FFI wrappers behind the local-embeddings feature flag, not part of the core.

Full comparison with benchmarks →

Architecture — Crate Map

Core (always compiled):

Crate LoC Role
moltis (cli) 4.0K Entry point, CLI commands
moltis-agents 9.6K Agent loop, streaming, prompt assembly
moltis-providers 17.6K LLM provider implementations
moltis-gateway 36.1K HTTP/WS server, RPC, auth
moltis-chat 11.5K Chat engine, agent orchestration
moltis-tools 21.9K Tool execution, sandbox
moltis-config 7.0K Configuration, validation
moltis-sessions 3.8K Session persistence
moltis-plugins 1.9K Hook dispatch, plugin formats
moltis-service-traits 1.3K Shared service interfaces
moltis-common 1.1K Shared utilities
moltis-protocol 0.8K Wire protocol types

Optional (feature-gated or additive):

Category Crates Combined LoC
Web UI moltis-web 4.5K
GraphQL moltis-graphql 4.8K
Voice moltis-voice 6.0K
Memory moltis-memory, moltis-qmd 5.9K
Channels moltis-telegram, moltis-whatsapp, moltis-discord, moltis-msteams, moltis-channels 14.9K
Browser moltis-browser 5.1K
Scheduling moltis-cron, moltis-caldav 5.2K
Extensibility moltis-mcp, moltis-skills, moltis-wasm-tools 9.1K
Auth & Security moltis-auth, moltis-oauth, moltis-onboarding, moltis-vault 6.6K
Networking moltis-network-filter, moltis-tls, moltis-tailscale 3.5K
Provider setup moltis-provider-setup 4.3K
Import moltis-openclaw-import 7.6K
Apple native moltis-swift-bridge 2.1K
Metrics moltis-metrics 1.7K
Other moltis-projects, moltis-media, moltis-routing, moltis-canvas, moltis-auto-reply, moltis-schema-export, moltis-benchmarks 2.5K

Use --no-default-features --features lightweight for constrained devices (Raspberry Pi, etc.).

Security

  • Zero unsafe code* — denied workspace-wide; only opt-in FFI behind local-embeddings flag
  • Sandboxed execution — Docker + Apple Container, per-session isolation
  • Secret handling — secrecy::Secret, zeroed on drop, redacted from tool output
  • Authentication — password + passkey (WebAuthn), rate-limited, per-IP throttle
  • SSRF protection — DNS-resolved, blocks loopback/private/link-local
  • Origin validation — rejects cross-origin WebSocket upgrades
  • Hook gating — BeforeToolCall hooks can inspect/block any tool invocation

See Security Architecture for details.

Features

  • AI Gateway — Multi-provider LLM support (OpenAI Codex, GitHub Copilot, Local), streaming responses, agent loop with sub-agent delegation, parallel tool execution
  • Communication — Web UI, Telegram, Microsoft Teams, Discord, API access, voice I/O (8 TTS + 7 STT providers), mobile PWA with push notifications
  • Memory & Context — Per-agent memory workspaces, embeddings-powered long-term memory, hybrid vector + full-text search, session persistence with auto-compaction, project context
  • Extensibility — MCP servers (stdio + HTTP/SSE), skill system, 15 lifecycle hook events with circuit breaker, destructive command guard
  • Security — Encryption-at-rest vault (XChaCha20-Poly1305 + Argon2id), password + passkey + API key auth, sandbox isolation, SSRF/CSWSH protection
  • Operations — Cron scheduling, OpenTelemetry tracing, Prometheus metrics, cloud deploy (Fly.io, DigitalOcean), Tailscale integration

How It Works

Moltis is a local-first AI gateway — a single Rust binary that sits between you and multiple LLM providers. Everything runs on your machine; no cloud relay required.

┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐
│   Web UI    │  │  Telegram   │  │  Discord    │
└──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘
       │                │                │
       └────────┬───────┴────────┬───────┘
                │   WebSocket    │
                ▼                ▼
        ┌─────────────────────────────────┐
        │          Gateway Server         │
        │   (Axum · HTTP · WS · Auth)     │
        ├─────────────────────────────────┤
        │        Chat Service             │
        │  ┌───────────┐ ┌─────────────┐  │
        │  │   Agent   │ │    Tool     │  │
        │  │   Runner  │◄┤   Registry  │  │
        │  └─────┬─────┘ └─────────────┘  │
        │        │                        │
        │  ┌─────▼─────────────────────┐  │
        │  │    Provider Registry      │  │
        │  │  Multiple providers       │  │
        │  │  (Codex · Copilot · Local)│  │
        │  └───────────────────────────┘  │
        ├─────────────────────────────────┤
        │  Sessions  │ Memory  │  Hooks   │
        │  (JSONL)   │ (SQLite)│ (events) │
        └─────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
               ┌───────▼───────┐
               │    Sandbox    │
               │ Docker/Apple  │
               │  Container    │
               └───────────────┘

See Quickstart for gateway startup, message flow, sessions, and memory details.

Getting Started

Build & Run

Requires just (command runner) and Node.js (for Tailwind CSS).

git clone https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis.git
cd moltis
just build-css                  # Build Tailwind CSS for the web UI
just build-release              # Build in release mode
cargo run --release --bin moltis

For a full release build including WASM sandbox tools:

just build-release-with-wasm    # Builds WASM artifacts + release binary
cargo run --release --bin moltis

Open https://moltis.localhost:3000. On first run, a setup code is printed to the terminal — enter it in the web UI to set your password or register a passkey.

Optional flags: --config-dir /path/to/config --data-dir /path/to/data

Docker

# Docker / OrbStack
docker run -d \
  --name moltis \
  -p 13131:13131 \
  -p 13132:13132 \
  -p 1455:1455 \
  -v moltis-config:/home/moltis/.config/moltis \
  -v moltis-data:/home/moltis/.moltis \
  -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \
  ghcr.io/moltis-org/moltis:latest

Open https://localhost:13131 and complete the setup. See Docker docs for Podman, OrbStack, TLS trust, and persistence details.
